hero.hero_overview:
Dyrroth is a fighter with high sustain and massive burst damage, especially after his revamp, which made his skills faster and more effective in 1v1 duels. As an EXP lane hero, Dyrroth excels at countering enemy fighters and exerting pressure early in the game.
Dyrroth can utilize Skill 2 (Spectre Step) to open up fights and Skill 1 (Burst Strike) to deal area damage and regenerate HP through his passive. His ultimate, Abysm Strike, delivers a massive burst that can penetrate shields and kill low-level heroes.
hero.hero_strength:
✅ 1v1 Dominator: Dyrroth can defeat many EXP fighters like X.Borg, Terizla, and Lapu-Lapu with the right skill timing.
✅ High Sustain: His passive "Wrath of the Abyss" grants him massive HP regen after using the skill twice.
✅ Burst Damage: The combination of Skill 2 + Ultimate can instantly take down squishy heroes like marksmen or mages.
✅ Good Mobility: Skill 2 makes it easy to get in and out of teamfights.
✅ Effective in the Early-Mid Game: Fast power spike, very powerful after the first item (War Axe or Bloodlust Axe) is equipped.
hero.hero_weakness:
❌ Vulnerable to Crowd Control: Easily countered by high-CC heroes like Khufra, Tigreal, or Chou.
❌ Weak in the Late Game: His damage decreases against heavy tanks or high-lifesteal heroes.
❌ Dependent on Skill Timing: Mispositioning or misusing skills can easily kill him.
❌ Difficult Against Range Heroes: Ranged marksmen or mages can poke him before he enters combat.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Focus: farm safely and fill Rage by hitting minions; trade only when you have at least one Rage bar. Look for level advantages at level 4/5. 

Use S2 to close distance, S1 to proc damage, and back off to heal on minions if necessary. Do not overextend without vision.

If you are jungling, secure early objectives (turtle) with Retribution.

Start grouping for picks — Dyrroth shines in isolated fights. With items like Heptaseas + Hunter Strike you can burst squishies and force fights.

Split-push if enemy team cannot match you 1v1; otherwise, look for flank engages with Flicker.

Your job: Catch and eliminate priority squishy targets or threaten the backline. Do not dive blindly into multi-CC. If team comp lacks front-line, consider ordering Immortality earlier. Use Sea Halberd vs heavy healing comps.

Always watch your Rage bars before committing. A full Rage drastically changes trading windows. 

Avoid fights in the enemy's turret range unless you are certain of a kill/escape - Dyrroth lacks instant invulnerability.

Buy anti-heal early (Sea Halberd) vs sustain comps — it neuters his + enemy sustain. 

Build defensively (Dominance Ice / Immortality) if enemy burst or CC is overwhelming late game.

emblems.emblem_desc: Recommended Emblem: Assassin (or Fighter if you prefer survivability). Assassin gives extra penetration and one-versus-one damage that synergizes with Dyrroth's combo. Pick Thrill / Lethal Ignition and Master Assassin for dueling power.

equips.equip_desc:
1. Tough Boots (magic/tenacity depending on enemy CC) — early sustain and resist. 


2. Blade of the Heptaseas — good early burst and lane-snowball when combined with his on-hit playstyle. 


3. Sky Piercer (or a similar attack speed/penetration item) — enhances single-target damage and chase. 


4. Hunter Strike — mobility and burst follow-up to stick to targets. 


5. Sea Halberd (anti-heal) — must-buy vs sustain champs (Esmeralda, Yu Zhong, heroes with lifesteal). 


6. Blade of Despair (finish) — huge AD power spike to carry fights.
Alternative: Dominance Ice or Immortality if team needs anti-heal/defense or survivability.

hero.hero_overview:
Dyrroth is a melee fighter (EXP lane / offlane / occasional jungler) who excels at single-target burst + sustained duels. His kit rewards trading near minion waves (sustain) and hitting enemies with a two-bar 'Rage' enhancement for stronger follow-up skills. He is built to snowball lane fights into map pressure and solo kills. 



SKILLS:

Passive — Wrath of the Abyss / Rage mechanic: Dyrroth gains sustain/heal on hits and fills Rage bars when hitting minions/targets. Enhanced skills consume Rage for improved effects. Use lane minions to maximize sustain. 

Skill 1 — Burst Strike / Circle Strike (spam / poke): AoE/line/multi-hit attack that procs on-hit effects (and crits). Use to farm, proc Rage, and weave into auto-attacks for max damage. 

Skill 2 — Spectre Step (gap-closer): Dashes/steps toward target, deals damage and can be enhanced if Rage available (longer range / extra effect). Use to start trades or chase. 
Ultimate — Abysm Strike (big execute/burst): High-damage finisher that combos well after S1+S2; it can be a gap closer or execute depending on enhancement state. Missing it or getting interrupted leaves you exposed. 



SKILL COMBO:

1. Fill Rage (hit minions / trade).


2. S2 (Spectre Step) >> S1 (Burst Strike) >> Auto-attack ×2 >> Ultimate (Abysm Strike) >> chase/basic attacks.
Enhanced skills when Rage full add burst or punch-through — plan your engage around Rage.
hero.hero_strength:
Exceptional 1v1 dueling and sustain when he triggers his passive near minions. 

Strong single-target burst with combo windows when Rage bars are available. 

Great lane pressure — can convert early solo kills into tower lead and map control.
hero.hero_weakness:
Vulnerable to heavy crowd control and reliable displace/interrupts (knockups, stuns, silences). 

Falls off against teams that build heavy anti-heal / anti-lifesteal or stack durable frontlines without easy picks. 

Relies on having minion waves or Rage bars; poor Rage management or bad positioning can make engages fail.

dominants.dominant_desc:
🌟 Rage Management is Key: Always prioritize filling your Rage Bar (Passive) by hitting minions/creeps before engaging the enemy hero.

🔥 Goal: Ensure your Skill 1 and Skill 2 are enhanced for the massive damage, larger area, and higher defense shred.

🌟 Lane Bully Tactics: Dyrroth is an early-game fighter. Use your enhanced skills to constantly poke and trade with the enemy EXP laner.

🔥 Poke Combo: Use the enhanced Skill 1 (Burst Strike) from a safe distance to slow and chip their HP.

🌟 Sustain with Passive: Use your passive's Circle Strike (triggered after 2 basic attacks) on minion waves or heroes to regenerate HP, allowing you to stay in lane longer.

🌟 Roam after Wave: After quickly clearing the lane with enhanced skills, check the map. If the Mid Lane or Jungle needs help, a quick roam can secure an early kill/objective.

🌟 CC is Your Enemy: Dyrroth is vulnerable to Crowd Control (CC). Avoid committing to trades when you know the enemy has stuns (e.g., Chou, Guinevere) ready.

🌟 Watch the Map: Dyrroth has poor escape after committing. If you see two or more enemies missing, retreat immediately to avoid ganks.

🌟 Don't Waste S2 Dash: Your Skill 2 (first dash) is your only blink/escape. Use it for offense only when you're confident in getting the kill or your surroundings are safe.

🌟 Ultimate Timing: Don't use your Ultimate (Abysm Strike) too early. It deals more damage based on the enemy's missing HP, so save it as an execution tool to secure the kill.

🌟 The Flanker/Diver: Your primary role is to dive the enemy backline (Mage/MM) and burst them down, or shred the frontline (Tank/Fighter) for your team.

🌟 Initiation Combo (Backline Dive)

🔥 Flank from the side or behind, never front-to-back unless the enemy backline is isolated.

🔥 Use the enhanced Skill 2 combo on the highest priority squishy target (MM/Mage).

🔥 Follow up with enhanced Skill 1.

🔥Finish with Ultimate (Abysm Strike).

🌟 Against Tanky Comps: Dyrroth is a natural tank counter due to his defense shred. Use the enhanced Skill 2 on the enemy frontline to open them up for your Jungler and Marksman's physical damage.

🌟 Flicker + Ultimate Trick: You can use Flicker during the charge of your Ultimate to instantly reposition and land it on a fleeing or hard-to-reach target.

🌟 Peeling: If your team's Marksman is being focused, save your Skill 2 (Fatal Strike) to apply the massive slow and defense shred on the enemy diving your carry, turning the tables.

🌟 Hit-and-Run: Once your core combo is executed, if the fight is still going, fall back slightly to regenerate HP with your passive on a nearby minion or creep before re-engaging.

emblems.emblem_desc:
🌟 Dyrroth is primarily a burst hero, and the Assassin Emblem maximizes his damage output and provides essential mobility.

🔥 Rupture: Provides consistent physical penetration.

🔥 Master Assasin: Boosts his damage when he is fighting a single enemy (1v1 duels in EXP lane or ganking a solo backliner), which is Dyrroth's specialty.

🔥 Lethal Ignition: Provides a delayed burst of damage after landing multiple hits. This synergizes perfectly with Dyrroth's multi-hit Skill 1 and full combo, helping to secure the kill.

equips.equip_desc:
🌟 This build focuses on early flat penetration, cooldown, and late-game survivability, allowing you to dominate the lane and burst squishies.

🔥 Tough Boots: CC Duration Reduction (Essential defense).

🔥 Blade of Heptaseas: High Flat Physical PEN and burst damage on the first basic attack (great for his combo).

🔥 Hunter Strike: Physical PEN, Damage, and Movement Speed burst on hit (key for chasing/escaping).

🔥 War Axe: Sustain Damage and Movement Speed (synergizes with his high-hit kit).

🔥 Sea Halberd: Anti-heal vs. high-HP Regen heroes.

🔥 Blade of Despair: Massive Physical Attack boost for finishing targets with the Ultimate.

hero.hero_overview:
👑 Background

✨ Prince of the Moniyan Empire, who was kidnapped and raised in the Abyss by Alice and Thamuz.

✨ Known as the "Prince of the Abyss."

👑 Overview

✨ A relentless Fighter hero specializing in Charge and Burst damage.

✨ Excels in one-on-one combat and bullying opponents in the lane due to strong early-game damage and sustain.

✨ His key mechanic is his enhanced skills activated by his Passive, which also triggers an area-of-effect (AoE) attack with lifesteal.

✨ His primary asset is his Spectre Step (Skill 2), which, when enhanced, significantly reduces the target's Physical Defense by up to 75%, making him a potent counter to tanky heroes.

👑 Role in the Game:

✨ EXP Laner: His primary role, where he dominates the lane, pressures the enemy laner, and creates a gold/EXP advantage. He uses his superior laning power to rotate for ganks or objective control.

✨ Jungler: A viable secondary role where he utilizes the Retribution battle spell to clear jungle camps quickly and secure major objectives (Turtle/Lord), leveraging his burst damage for early-game kills.

✨ His main function is to dive into the enemy backline to eliminate "squishy" damage dealers (Mages, Marksmen) using his high burst damage and defense-shredding abilities.
hero.hero_strength:
👑 LANING/FARMING

🔥 Dominating Laning Phase: Extremely strong early-game presence. His burst damage and sustain allow him to easily bully and zone out most opposing EXP laners.

🔥 Exceptional Sustain: His Passive (Wrath of the Abyss) grants him an AoE attack (Circle Strike) that restores HP, making him very durable during trades and while clearing minion waves/jungle creeps.

🔥 Defense Shred (Anti-Tank): His Spectre Step (Skill 2), especially when enhanced, shreds an extreme amount of the target's Physical Defense (up to 75%), making him an excellent "tank-buster" in a 1v1.

👑 TEAM FIGHT

🔥 Backline Dive/Burst: Excels at a hit-and-run, Assassin-like playstyle. He can use Spectre Step to bypass the frontline and instantly delete squishy enemy Mages or Marksmen.

🔥 Objective Control (Lord/Turtle): His high Physical Damage and defense-shred capability allow his team to take objectives faster, especially if he can land his Spectre Step on the objective.

🔥 Finishing Power: His Ultimate (Abysm Strike) deals extra damage based on the target's missing HP, making it a powerful execution tool to secure kills in a chaotic fight.

👑 HERO CHARACTERISTICS 

🔥 Strong Snowball Potential: If Dyrroth gains an early lead, his damage becomes overwhelming, allowing him to carry the game and prevent the enemy team from scaling.

🔥 Versatile Build Paths: Can be built as a full-damage assassin to maximize his burst, or as a Bruiser (hybrid damage/defense) to increase his survivability in extended fights.
hero.hero_weakness:
👑 LANING/FARMING

🔥 Vulnerable to Poke/Range: Heroes who can constantly poke or clear waves from a safe distance can prevent him from engaging and building up his Passive.

🔥 Relies on Aggression: To maintain his strength and clear speed, he needs to constantly land attacks to trigger his Passive and refresh his skill cooldowns. Passive/defensive play hinders his potential.

🔥 Susceptible to Lane Freezing: A skilled enemy laner can freeze the lane near their turret, denying Dyrroth the safe space to fully commit to an all-in attack.

👑 TEAM FIGHT

🔥 Low Team Fight Utility: Aside from a slow from his skills, he offers very little Crowd Control (CC) or utility. His main role is single-target elimination, not group setup.

🔥 Highly Punishable All-In: His main engagement tool (Spectre Step) is also his only reliable disengage. Once he commits, he is fully vulnerable to CC and concentrated enemy fire.

🔥 Vulnerable to Hard CC: Being an all-in hero, Dyrroth is easily shut down by hard CC (Stun, Suppression, Knock-up) as he relies on cycling his skills to survive and deal damage.

👑 HERO CHARACTERISTICS 

🔥 Falls Off Late Game: Compared to hyper-carry Marksmen or scaling Fighters, Dyrroth's pure burst damage can struggle in the very late game against full defensive builds and constant protection from enemy supports.

🔥 Needs Precise Targeting: Missing the second phase of Spectre Step means losing the massive defense shred, severely crippling his burst combo and making his all-in much less effective.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Tigreal and Dyrroth form an excellent combination due to their synergy in crowd control and burst follow-up. Tigreal's Initiate + Knock-up combo (Implosion) gathers enemies in one area, setting up Dyrroth's Abysm Strike to hit multiple targets at once. This combo can instantly wipe out the enemy backline if executed properly. Tigreal also acts as a strong frontliner, absorbing incoming damage while Dyrroth waits for the perfect opportunity to dive. With Tigreal's peel and setup abilities, Dyrroth can focus purely on executing high-priority targets without worrying about being crowd-controlled early in the fight. This duo thrives in team-based skirmishes and Lord fights, where their combination of control and burst can instantly swing the momentum in their team's favor.

Angela's Heartguard (Ultimate) perfectly complements Dyrroth's aggressive playstyle. Once Dyrroth dives into the enemy backline using Spectre Step, Angela can attach to him and provide constant healing and shield protection, making him far more durable in sustained fights. Angela's CC and slowing skills also help Dyrroth land his combos more easily, especially when chasing mobile heroes. The combination allows Dyrroth to go all-in without fear of dying quickly. Moreover, Angela's global presence means she can protect Dyrroth no matter where he is on the map, making this duo strong for both pick-off plays and clutch team fights. When Dyrroth initiates fights, Angela can immediately enhance his survivability, allowing him to keep dealing consistent damage.

Pharsa's long-range burst and zone control synergize perfectly with Dyrroth's assassination potential. While Pharsa bombards enemies from afar using her Feathered Air Strike, Dyrroth can flank and clean up any stragglers who attempt to escape her range. The zoning effect of Pharsa's skills forces enemies to group together or flee into predictable paths, allowing Dyrroth to catch them off guard using Spectre Step and Abysm Strike. This pairing thrives in siege and defense situations, as Pharsa handles the poke damage while Dyrroth waits for a prime opportunity to dive in and finish off weakened targets. Additionally, Dyrroth's ability to break through enemy backlines balances Pharsa's vulnerability, offering her protection from assassins trying to dive.

counters.counter_desc: Khufra is one of Dyrroth's hardest counters because he completely shuts down Dyrroth's mobility. Dyrroth heavily relies on Spectre Step to engage and escape, but Khufra's Bouncing Ball ability prevents dashes, forcing Dyrroth to fight without his main mobility tool. When Dyrroth attempts to dive into the backline, Khufra can easily disrupt him with Tyrant's Revenge or Ultimate (Tyrant's Rage), locking him down for his teammates to burst. Khufra's tanky nature also makes it difficult for Dyrroth to kill him early in fights. Since Dyrroth performs best when he can freely reposition and strike squishy targets, Khufra's control and anti-dash mechanics can effectively neutralize him throughout the game. To deal with Khufra, Dyrroth must play patiently, waiting until the tank's abilities are on cooldown before committing to an engagement.

Chou is another nightmare matchup for Dyrroth due to his high mobility, crowd control, and single-target lockdown potential. Chou's Jeet Kune Do combo allows him to knock Dyrroth up before he can fully execute his burst combo, and The Way of Dragon (Ultimate) can kick Dyrroth away from his intended targets or straight into the enemy team. This makes it nearly impossible for Dyrroth to freely dive the backline when Chou is present. Moreover, Chou's durability with defensive items allows him to survive Dyrroth's damage long enough to retaliate. Skilled Chou players can also interrupt Dyrroth's Spectre Step or ultimate, completely turning the tide of battle. Against Chou, Dyrroth should avoid isolated duels and instead focus on team fights where Chou can't easily single him out.

Valir counters Dyrroth through his constant crowd control and knockback abilities. Dyrroth's entire playstyle revolves around diving in and sticking close to his target, but Valir's Searing Torrent pushes him away, completely interrupting his combo. Additionally, Valir's Vengeance Flame (Ultimate) removes slows and grants extra mobility, making it difficult for Dyrroth to finish him off. Even when Dyrroth tries to flank, Valir's AOE burn damage and crowd control zone the fighter out of team fights. Valir excels at countering melee heroes who rely on close combat, which makes him an excellent answer to Dyrroth's aggression. The longer the fight goes on, the worse it gets for Dyrroth since Valir can continuously poke and reposition safely while dealing sustained damage.

dominants.dominant_desc:
1. Master his passive, Wrath of the Abyss.
Every time Dyrroth hits enemies with basic attacks or skills, he charges his Abyssal form. When it activates, his next skill becomes stronger—use this timing to maximize burst damage.


2. Use Skill 1 to poke and trigger Heptaseas.
Burst Strike is perfect for harassing enemies and activating the bonus damage from Blade of the Heptaseas. Try to hit enemies from max range to reduce their HP before engaging.


3. Time your Skill 2 (Spectre Step) wisely.
Don't rush both dashes immediately—use the first to close the gap, and the enhanced second dash (Abyss Enhanced) to burst enemies. It's both an engage and an escape tool, so think ahead before using it.


4. Combo efficiently.
Best combo: Skill 1 then Basic Attack then Skill 2 (1st Dash) then Skill 2 (Enhanced Dash) then Ultimate.
This ensures maximum damage while keeping your passive active throughout the fight.


5. Take advantage of his lifesteal build.
With items like Queen's Wings and Bloodlust Axe, Dyrroth can heal a lot during fights. Don't be afraid to sustain in combat as long as your skills are hitting.


6. Target the enemy backline.
Your goal is to eliminate squishy heroes like marksmen and mages. Wait for tanks to engage, then flank from the sides to take out key damage dealers.


7. Avoid crowd control.
Dyrroth is strong in 1v1 fights but vulnerable to CC. Consider using Purify or Tough Boots if the enemy lineup has many stuns or roots.


8. Use your Ultimate (Abysm Strike) for finishers.
Dyrroth's Ultimate deals more damage based on the enemy's missing HP. Use it as an execute to secure kills or to turn around fights when enemies are retreating.


9. Rotate aggressively in mid game.
Once you have two core items (Heptaseas + Hunter Strike), start roaming for kills. Dyrroth's burst potential shines most in 2v2 or 3v3 skirmishes.


10. Position like an assassin, fight like a fighter.
Don't dive blindly into team fights. Wait for the perfect moment to enter.

Early Game (Levels 1-5)

In the early stages, Dyrroth thrives in the EXP lane where he can take advantage of his strong poke and sustain. Use Skill 1 (Burst Strike) to clear waves efficiently and harass melee opponents from a safe distance. Each time you land a skill or basic attack, your Wrath of the Abyss passive charges—take note of this since it strengthens your next ability. Focus on last-hitting minions to get your core items faster, such as Blade of the Heptaseas for early burst. If you are the jungler, use Skill 2 (Spectre Step) to quickly move between camps and secure the Turtle when possible. Always watch your positioning; Dyrroth is aggressive but still fragile early on, so avoid unnecessary 1v2s.


Mid Game (Levels 6-11)

This is where Dyrroth becomes truly dangerous. With Heptaseas and Hunter Strike (or Bloodlust Axe if you prefer sustain), your damage spikes significantly. Begin to rotate aggressively—don't stay in lane for too long. Dyrroth's mobility and burst allow him to ambush squishy targets like mages and marksmen. Take advantage of brushes and fog of war to surprise enemies, starting your combo with Skill 2's first dash, then using the enhanced version to gap close or finish them off. You can also help your team contest objectives by zoning out the enemy backline using Abysm Strike, which not only deals huge damage but also reduces enemy defense.


Late Game (Levels 12-15)

In the late game, one mistake can cost your team everything, so be smart with your engagements. Dyrroth's burst remains lethal, but enemies will likely have defensive items or CC abilities ready for you. Wait for the right moment—usually after enemy tanks have used their crowd control—then dive onto the backline. Always target the damage dealers first, because eliminating them quickly can shift the fight in your team's favor. Use Queen's Wings and Bloodlust Axe for survivability and lifesteal so you can sustain longer in fights.

emblems.emblem_desc:
The best emblem for Dyrroth is the Assassin Emblem.  This emblem helps him amplify his damage output, especially in duels and pickoffs, which fits his role as a fighter who thrives on burst + sustain tradeoffs.

Suggested Talent Path (Assassin Emblem)

Here are the recommended talents from each tier and how they synergize with Dyrroth's kit:

Tier 1: Thrill
This talent gives you extra adaptive attack (bonus damage). Dyrroth wants to maximize his damage from basic attacks and skills, so that extra adaptive attack helps him scale stronger in early trades. 

Tier 2: Master Assassin
This talent increases your damage in 1v1 situations. Since Dyrroth often finds himself dueling enemies in the EXP lane, this talent empowers his ability to win those isolated fights. 

Tier 3 (Core): Lethal Ignition
This talent adds burn damage on multiple hits (multiple basic attacks or skills). Because Dyrroth triggers multiple hits via enhanced skills and his passive Circle Strike, Lethal Ignition amplifies his sustained damage over a fight. It makes his combos more punishing. 


Why This Emblem Setup Works Well

Damage Amplification: Since Dyrroth's power comes from combining skills, basic attacks, and his passive, the extra adaptive attack and penetration help all parts of his kit deal more damage.

Duels / 1v1 Superiority: With Master Assassin, Dyrroth becomes very dangerous in isolated fights, which is where he often wins lane matchups.

Synergy with Multi-Hit Mechanics: Lethal Ignition's burn effect triggers well when Dyrroth uses multiple hits (e.g. Burst Strike, enhanced Spectre Step, Circle Strike). It adds bonus damage on top of his normal burst and helps finish off enemies.

Mobility & Execution: While this emblem doesn't provide direct mobility, the increased damage allows Dyrroth to be more confident in diving, chasing, or finishing off fleeing targets.

The Fighter Emblem is the best choice for players who want to maximize Dyrroth's sustain and durability during extended fights. It enhances his spell vamp, defense, and overall damage — perfect for his aggressive playstyle and constant trading in lane.

Recommended Setup:

Main Emblem: Fighter Emblem

Talent 1: Firmness - Increases Physical Attack, allowing Dyrroth to deal more damage with both his skills and basic attacks. This helps him dominate early-game trades and push waves faster.

Talent 2: Festival of Blood - The most important talent for this setup. It gives spell vamp, which lets Dyrroth heal himself whenever he deals skill damage. The more kills or assists he gets, the higher the spell vamp bonus becomes. This is essential for sustaining in fights and surviving ganks.

Talent 3: Brave Smite - Restores HP every time Dyrroth uses a skill that hits an enemy. This pairs extremely well with Festival of Blood, giving him double healing sources that make him very difficult to kill in team fights.


Why This Emblem Works:
This emblem turns Dyrroth into a relentless fighter who can dive into the middle of battles and sustain through damage. The combined healing from Festival of Blood and Brave Smite allows him to continuously engage without relying too much on lifesteal items. It makes him self-sufficient even when facing poke-heavy or sustain matchups.

This setup is especially powerful in 1v1 duels or prolonged skirmishes where Dyrroth can fully utilize his passive "Wrath of the Abyss." Every enhanced skill hit regenerates a significant portion of HP, allowing him to outlast most fighters in his lane.

If you want a more durable, lane-controlling Dyrroth who can stay on the map longer and doesn't rely heavily on items for sustain, the Fighter Emblem with Festival of Blood and Brave Smite is the most effective choice.

equips.equip_desc:
Dyrroth excels as a burst fighter who dominates in close-range combat with high physical penetration and lifesteal. His build focuses on enhancing his damage, mobility, and survivability, allowing him to both initiate and sustain fights effectively. Below is a detailed and balanced build that works well in both ranked and competitive play:

1. Tough Boots

Tough Boots are ideal when facing heavy magic burst or crowd control heroes like Eudora, Aurora, or Kagura. The extra magic defense and crowd control reduction help Dyrroth survive early ganks.


2. Blade of the Heptaseas

The passive effect deals bonus damage when you haven't taken or dealt damage for a short time — perfect for Dyrroth's ambush playstyle. When paired with his Skill 1 (Burst Strike), this item helps you burst down squishy targets instantly.


3. Hunter Strike

The CDR lets you spam your skills more frequently, while the movement speed bonus after hitting enemies makes it easier to chase or escape. This item works perfectly in mid-game fights when Dyrroth begins roaming and looking for solo pickoffs.


4. Sea Halberd

It provides solid attack stats along with a passive that reduces the healing and shield effects of enemies hit by your attacks. Since Dyrroth can continuously deal damage through his skills, this item ensures that enemy sustain heroes have a harder time recovering in battle.


5. Queen's Wings

The passive, which reduces incoming damage and gives additional spell vamp when your HP drops below 40%, synergizes perfectly with Dyrroth's skill set. It allows you to survive longer in fights and even turn the tide when low on health.

6. Immortality

It grants extra HP and physical defense, plus the passive resurrection effect which allows you to re-enter the fight after being taken down. This is particularly useful in clutch team fights, giving your team a second chance to win even if you get focused early. Dyrroth can use his revived form to finish off weakened enemies or retreat safely.

hero.hero_overview:
Dyrroth, known as the Prince of the Abyss, was born into the Moniyan Empire but was kidnapped early in life. He was raised by Alice and Thamuz in the abyss, trained in darkness and strategy. Over time, he embraced the power of the Abyss, mastering deception, combat, and royal ambition. He now fights to implement a grand scheme known as the "Dawn Plan," turning his fate into one of conquest and chaos. 

Release Date & Price

Release Date: June 25, 2019 

Cost: 32,000 Battle Points or 599 Diamonds 


Role & Specialty

Role: Fighter 

Specialty: Charge / Burst 

Lanes: EXP Lane (primary) and can also be used Jungle in some setups 


Passive: Wrath of the Abyss

Accumulates Rage as you attack enemies and use skills. Once Rage reaches certain thresholds (bars), your Burst Strike and Spectre Step get enhanced versions. 

After every two basic attacks, Dyrroth releases Circle Strike, dealing area physical damage and regenerating HP based on damage dealt. 

Skill 1: Burst Strike

Fires a directional burst dealing physical damage and and applying a slow to enemies hit. 

Its enhanced version (when Rage is filled) extends range, increases damage, and doubles slow effect.

Damage decays if hitting the same target repeatedly; damage to minions is reduced.


Skill 2: Spectre Step

First cast: Dyrroth dashes forward, dealing physical damage to enemies in path. 

Second cast (Fatal Strike): Locks onto an enemy and hits them multiple times, also reducing their physical defense for several seconds. 

In its enhanced version, the damage is increased, slows more, and the defense reduction is stronger. 


Ultimate: Abysm Strike

Dyrroth charges and unleashes a powerful slash in a direction, dealing physical damage. Part of the damage scales with the target's missing HP (a finishing mechanic). 

It also applies a slow to hit enemies, helping him stick and finish off targets. 

Because of the scaling with missing HP, Abysm Strike is ideal for executing weakened foes.
hero.hero_strength:
Dyrroth excels as a close range fighter who thrives in extended combat and sustained pressure situations. His passive skill Wrath of the Abyss gives him strong self sustain through Circle Strike which restores his health every few hits and reduces cooldowns of his main skills. This makes him very effective in long trades against other fighters or junglers. His kit also allows him to constantly engage and disengage thanks to Spectre Step giving him high mobility and chase potential.

In laning phase Dyrroth can easily dominate the Exp lane because his Burst Strike has long range and area damage that allows him to clear waves fast and harass opponents safely. Once his passive activates he can out sustain most enemies in early fights. He is also strong at invading or punishing slow junglers because of his fast cooldowns and strong burst potential.

During team fights Dyrroth can play both as a finisher and front line disruptor. His ultimate Abysm Strike is especially deadly since it deals more damage based on the target's missing health, allowing him to quickly execute low hp enemies or carry heroes hiding behind tanks. His Spectre Step also gives him good positioning power to dive into the backline or escape once the fight turns against him.

Dyrroth performs best when paired with heroes who can provide crowd control or sustain to let him freely deal damage. He can initiate skirmishes, chase escaping enemies, and dominate drawn out fights due to his ability to constantly deal damage while recovering his own health. His damage spikes early in the game which makes him a great tempo hero capable of snowballing and taking control before the enemy cores scale.
hero.hero_weakness:
Despite his strong offensive power, Dyrroth has several weaknesses that players must manage carefully to maximize his potential. One of his biggest downsides is his lack of crowd control immunity or defensive tools. He relies purely on mobility and lifesteal for survival, which means that once he gets hit by stuns, silences, or knock ups, he can be easily bursted down before using his regeneration or dashes effectively. This makes him very vulnerable to heroes with strong crowd control abilities or sudden burst damage.

Another weakness of Dyrroth is his dependence on close range combat. Most of his skills require him to be in melee distance, and his Burst Strike, while ranged, loses power with every enemy hit, reducing its impact in crowded fights. Against heroes with long range pokes or zoning abilities, Dyrroth struggles to close the gap safely. He heavily depends on timing his Spectre Step and enhanced skills to make meaningful trades; missing a key skill or using Spectre Step recklessly can lead to instant punishment.

In terms of farming and scaling, Dyrroth can fall off if he doesn't secure early kills or objectives. His power peaks in the early to mid game, but he becomes less dominant once enemy cores build defensive items or crowd control tools to counter his aggression. He also has trouble fighting against tanky heroes with sustain or shields since his kit lacks pure burst after his combo.

Team fight positioning is another major challenge. Dyrroth tends to dive into the enemy backline, which often leaves him isolated and focused down if his team cannot follow up. He needs proper coordination and timing to enter fights safely. Lastly, his sustain from Circle Strike depends on hitting multiple enemies, meaning that in 1v1 fights against burst assassins or duelists who can avoid his skill hits, his regeneration becomes unreliable. These weaknesses make him highly reliant on skill accuracy, map awareness, and timing to perform well in any match.

data.recommend: Dyrroth shines with perfect timing and precision—play aggressively but wisely, and he'll dominate every battle.

spell.spell_desc:
Dyrroth's optimal battle spell depends on his role (EXP laner or jungler). Two spells stand out: Flicker and Retribution. Each has different use cases, advantages, and strategic impacts.


Why use Flicker?

As an EXP laner, Dyrroth often faces enemy roamers, ganks, or needs to chase escaping foes. Flicker gives him instant repositioning, allowing him to extend the range or redirect his ultimate (Abysm Strike) mid-cast, catching enemies off guard. 

It also provides a survival escape, helping him dodge enemy crowd control or burst damage during teamfights. Guides mention pairing Flicker with his ultimate to surprise enemies or correct mispositioning. 

Many pro Dyrroth builds and guides for lane role recommend Flicker as the top choice because of its versatility in both offense and defense. 


Usage Tips

Initiate Abysm Strike, then Flicker toward your intended direction if the target is moving; this helps land the ultimate more reliably. 

Use Flicker to dodge deadly CC or engage after your tank or support has opened the fight, giving you safer entry.

Always keep Flicker available when diving or forced fights, especially late game when death can decide the match.


Retribution (Recommended for Jungle)

Why use Retribution?

If Dyrroth is played in the jungle, Retribution is essential for faster jungle clear, securing buffs, and contesting objectives like Turtle and Lord. 

It allows him to maintain pace with opposing junglers and aids in ganks by giving him reliable gold and experience gain.


Usage Tips

Use Retribution on jungle camps or large monsters to speed up clears and maintain your gold/XP progression.

With proper upgrades (Ice Retribution, Bloody Retribution), you can use it offensively to steal buff monsters or finish off low-HP objectives.

In mid game, Retribution helps you join fights quicker by ensuring you stay relevant in gold and levels.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Early Game - Dominant

Dyrroth is one of the strongest heroes at Level 2 (with S1 and S2 unlocked) due to the immense burst potential from a successful S2 combo and his passive's sustain. Abuse this phase to bully the enemy EXP laner and control the gold crab.

Mid Game - Strong

With 2-3 damage items, he becomes a terrifying assassin for squishy targets. He excels at rotating to help secure the Turtle/Turrets or flanking to burst down enemy Marksmen/Mages.

Late Game - Conditional

His effectiveness hinges on his ability to flank and burst a back-liner before being caught by CC. If the enemy front-line is too tanky and positioned well, Dyrroth can struggle. His main role here is to shred the tank/fighter with S2 so the team can follow up.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Dyrroth is best suited for the Assassin Emblem to maximize his burst damage and movement speed for chasing and roaming.

Tier 1: Swift

Increases Attack Speed. Essential for quickly stacking his passive (Circle Strike) and enhancing his overall damage output in between skills.

Tier 2: Master Assassin

Increases damage when only one enemy hero is nearby. This is perfect for Dyrroth's specialty: 1v1 duels in the EXP Lane or picking off isolated squishies.

Tier 3: Lethal Ignition
After dealing damage to an enemy three times within 5 seconds, it burns the target for extra damage.

equips.equip_desc:
The core focus is on Physical Penetration to maximize the damage amplification from his S2's armor shred, combined with key utility items.

1. Tough Boots / Warrior Boots

Tough: Best against heavy CC and Magic Damage. Warrior: Best against Physical Damage laners/junglers.

2. War Axe

Provides Physical Attack, CD Reduction, and a unique passive that grants Physical Penetration and Movement Speed upon dealing damage, perfectly matching Dyrroth's sustained fighting style.

3. Hunter Strike

Huge Physical Attack, CD Reduction, and Physical Penetration. Its passive grants a burst of Movement Speed upon hitting an enemy hero 5 times, helping you chase or escape.
4	Blade of Despair (BoD)	Massive increase in Physical Attack. The passive grants even more Physical Attack when hitting targets below 50% HP, greatly enhancing his Abysm Strike (Ultimate) as a finisher.

5. Dominance Ice

Provides Armor, Mana Regen, CD Reduction, and an essential passive: Anti-Heal (reduces Lifesteal/Regen) and Attack Speed reduction for the enemy team. Great against high-sustain heroes.

6. Immortality

Provides a second life with a shield, which is crucial for a dive-heavy fighter. If the game ends quickly, it can be replaced with another damage item.

hero.hero_overview:
Role: Fighter

Specialty: Burst / Crowd Control

Lane: EXP Lane (Primary), Jungle (Secondary)

Core Mechanic: Rage System (Passive)
Dyrroth's two active skills, S1 (Burst Strike) and S2 (Spectre Step), are enhanced when his Rage bar is full, significantly increasing their damage, effect range, and slow.

Unique Power: Physical Defense Shred
His S2, Fatal Strike (second phase), reduces a target's Physical Defense by a massive percentage (up to 75%), making him an exceptional tank/fighter buster and greatly boosting the damage of his entire team's Physical damage dealers.
hero.hero_strength:
1. Early Game Dominance: High burst damage from S2 combo and enhanced S1 makes him one of the best 1v1 EXP laners.

2. Tank Shredder: His S2's armor-shredding effect is unparalleled, turning even the tankiest heroes into squishy targets.

3. Great Sustain: His passive, Circle Strike, triggers after every two basic attacks, dealing damage in an area and healing him based on the damage dealt, providing strong laning sustain.

4. Burst Finisher: His Ultimate, Abysm Strike, deals higher damage the lower the target's HP, making it a perfect execution tool.
hero.hero_weakness:
1. Vulnerable to Ganks: While S2 is a dash, it can be easily body-blocked or interrupted, making him easy to shut down if focused by multiple enemies.

2. Reliance on S2 Landing: His entire burst combo and physical defense shred depend on landing the second phase of S2 (Fatal Strike). A miss severely cripples his damage.

3. CC Vulnerability: He has no built-in Crowd Control (CC) immunity. Hard CC (Stuns, Suppress, Airborne) can completely interrupt his combo and leave him exposed.

4. Ultimate Predictability: His Ultimate has a noticeable charge-up animation, which good enemies can easily dodge with a simple dash or blink skill.

spell.spell_desc: Allows for critical repositioning to land the S2 combo, chase a fleeing low-HP target for an Ultimate finish, or, most commonly, to adjust the direction and range of his charged Abysm Strike for a surprise kill.

counters.counter_desc: - Tanks: Dyrroth is effective against tank heroes due to his second skill, Spectre Step, which can significantly reduce a target's physical defense. In Abyssal Mode, Spectre Step can reduce physical defense by 75%, making Dyrroth a strong counter to tanky heroes .

dominants.dominant_desc: - Late Game: During team fights, Dyrroth targets the enemy backlines or squishy heroes. Positioning is key to ensure his ultimate lands on multiple targets .
dominants.dominant_title: - Early Game: Dyrroth focuses on farming and poking his lane opponent with Skill 1 (Burst Strike). He looks for opportunities to secure kills when his rage meter is full .

emblems.emblem_desc: The Fighter emblem is a popular and effective choice for Dyrroth because it enhances his strengths as a durable, damage-dealing fighter .

equips.equip_desc: A sustain build on Dyrroth focuses on increasing his ability to stay alive and continue fighting for extended periods, rather than just bursting down enemies quickly. Here's why you might choose a sustain build for Dyrroth:

hero.hero_overview: Dyrroth is a Fighter hero known for his assassin-like playstyle, high physical damage, and strong durability. He excels in the EXP lane or jungle and is considered a medium-difficulty hero to master .
hero.hero_strength:
- High Attack Damage: Dyrroth boasts high physical attack damage, allowing him to deal significant damage to enemies .

- Escape Skill: His second skill, Specter Step, allows him to quickly dash away from danger, making him difficult to catch .

- Passive Skill: Wrath of the Abyss grants him enhanced abilities and bonus effects when his fury reaches 50%, enhancing his combat capabilities .

- Mobility: Dyrroth can quickly move around the battlefield, enabling him to engage or disengage from fights effectively .

- Durability: Despite his assassin-like playstyle, Dyrroth has good durability, making him resilient in prolonged engagements .

- Strong Against Tanks: Dyrroth's Specter Step skill can reduce a significant amount of the opponent's physical defense, making him effective against tank heroes .
hero.hero_weakness:
- Dependent on Skills: Dyrroth's damage output is heavily reliant on landing his skills, making him less effective if his skills are on cooldown or miss their targets .

- Vulnerable to Crowd Control: Dyrroth is very vulnerable to crowd control (CC) attacks, which can disrupt his combos and prevent him from dealing damage effectively .

- Weak Against Healing Reduction: Dyrroth's sustainability in fights is diminished when facing enemies with healing reduction abilities or items .

- No Lock Skill: Dyrroth lacks a reliable lock-on skill, making it difficult to target specific enemy heroes .
 
Skills
 
- Wrath of the Abyss (Passive): Enhances Burst Strike and Specter Step when fury reaches 50%. After every 2 basic attacks, Dyrroth unleashes Circle Attack, dealing physical damage and regenerating HP .

- Burst Strike (Skill 1): Dyrroth unleashes a burst blow, dealing physical damage and slowing enemies. When enhanced, it has a greater range and double the slowing effect .

- Specter Step (Skill 2): Dyrroth dashes in a designated direction, stopping when he hits a target. He then deals explosive damage. When enhanced, it deals increased damage, slows the target, and reduces their physical defense .

- Abyss Strike (Ultimate): Dyrroth charges and unleashes Abyss Strike, dealing massive physical damage plus additional damage based on the target's lost HP. It also slows the targets' movement speed .

emblems.emblem_desc:
يمتاز دايروث بين الابطال بشفاء مهاره عالي لذا اختيار مميزه المحارب ك مميزه اساسيه يعد متوازن بين انه يزيد من شفاء مهاراته ويزيد من الهجوم الجسدي المفقود من شراء بعض الدروع لدايروث 

بينما مميزه ك مهرجان الدماء كما قولنا من قبل تعمل علي زياده شفاء مهاراته بشكل ملحوظ 

اما عن النيران الغاشمه فهي تعد كومبو مثالي مع منجل الصياد فهيا تسبب دامج مستمر ومتفجر مع هذه المميزه و معده منجل الصياد

equips.equip_desc:

معدات منجل الصياد وفأس الحرب وحربه ملك البحار مع معدتان دفاع ولايف ستيل يحقق التوازن بين الضرر وشفاء المهاره

قمنا باستبدال كلا من العظام المشتعله وحربه ملك البحار بدرع اثينا ودومينك ايس 
وهنا السبب 
استبدلنا انتي هيل الهجوم بالدفاع عند مواجهه ابطال ك ارجوس او ثاموز او فريا ابطال دامج مستمر وسريع لذا دايروث يحتاج الي مواكبتهم عن طريق انتي هيل الدفاع 

بينما درع اثينا بالنسبة لي ببدا افكر اشتريه لو ضدي ميج دامج سحري ملحوظ في التيم فايت وفقط في التيم فايت + بطل ادغال دامج سحري لذا يجب مواكبة الدامج هذا عن طريق شراء درع اثينا او حتي الدرع المشع 

اما لو كان بطل الادغال ذو دامج جسدي قوي ومتفجر نستبدل اثينا ب الدرع العتيق وهذه في اسوأ الظروف
equips.equip_title: قد يستغني دايروث عن بعض الايتمات ويستبدلها حسب المباراه

hero.hero_overview:
حديثنا اليوم عن امير الهاويه الحالي ملك الظلام المستقبلي 
دايروث نعم يا عزيزي القارئ و القارئه دايروث ذي الدامج المتفجر والصمود المقبول 
يعد دايروث من اقوي الابطال في بدايه المباراه ان لم يكن الاقوي ومع بعض الخدع والمعدات سيكون قادر علي مواكبه المباراه الي النهاية 
في الآونة الأخيرة انقسم اللاعبين في موبايل ليجند حول طريقه لعب دايروث اما اكس بي مع تعويذه الانتقام وايتم فل تانك او جنجل فل دامج مع محاوله أنهاء المباراه في اسرع وقت 
انا في هذا المقال سأوضح لك مميزات وعيوب هذه الاستراتيجية والفت نظرك الي التوازن بينهم والآن فلنبدا
hero.hero_strength:
تكمن قوه دايروث في الباسيف او المهاره الخاصه وهيا تنقسم الي جزئين الجزئ الاول بعد كل ضربتين هجوم عادي يطلق دايروث هجوم واسع حول نفسه ويستعيد مقدار من نقاط حياته يعتم علي معداته وتجهيزاته 

الجزئ الثاني باسيف الغضب (غضب الهاويه) عند دايروث وهو عباره عن انه يوجد شريطين اسفل نقاط حياه الهيرو مع كل هجوم سواء بمهاره او تلقائي يزداد هذا الشريط وعند اكتماله يتم تحسين كلا من المهاره الاولي والثانيه لدايروث 
  المهاره الاولي يطلق دايروث مجموعه من كرات الطاقه مما تسبب ضرر للابطال يزداد بزياده المستوي وتعمل علي تبطئ الأعداء 
عند التحسين يزداد مدي هذه المهاره وتزداد نسبة التبطيئ للأعداء 
المهاره الثانيه خطوه الشبح يكمن داخلها قوه دايروث ب اكملها 
تنقسم الي جزئين 
يندفع دايروث الي مسافه معينه عند الاصطدام بشخص تدفعه قليلا الي الخلف وتسبب له ضرر 
عند الاستخدام مجددا يهجم دايروث علي العدو ب احدي شفراته مسببا له ضرر جسدي ويقلل من الدفاع الجسدي بنسبه 60% 
عند التحسين بغضب الهاويه يزداد مدي المهاره ويزداد الاختراق الجسدي من 60 الي 160%
hero.hero_weakness:
تكمن قوه دايروث في المهاره الثانيه التي بمقدرتها انا تقضي علي عدو او اكثر دون الحاجه الي المهاره القصوي 

وبالتالي يعد الابطال المضادين للداشات ك مينيستار وخوفرا يسببون لدايروث بعض المشاكل في هجوم الفريق 

أيضا دايروث من الابطال الاقويه واحد ضد واحد طول ما الخصم قدامه ودايروث يقدر يوصله بسهوله 

لذا يعد بطل ك وان وان او هارلي او اريثيل بالموجز الابطال ذوي الموبليتي العاليه  

يسببون مضايقه لدايروث لكن غلطه بسيطه تؤدي الي انهائه حياتك بالطبع

بالمختصر حاول الابتعاد عن مواجهه دايروث بمفردك اما ان تكون ذو حركه سريعه او ستموت ان تم الامساك بك

spell.spell_desc:
مخلب الادغال تعويذه مخصصه لابطال الادغال وبواقع ان من مستخدمي دايروث في الادغال ستكون محط حديثا اليوم 

التعويذه تحمل عدد من التراكمات تزداد عند قتل وحوش الادغال او المساعده بقتل عدو او قتله بنفسك تزداد التراكمات حتي 15 تراكم 

تنقسم مخلب الادغال الي ثلاثه انوع 

مخلب الجليد (ازرق اللون )
عند استخدماها علي احدي الاعداء تبطئ من سرعته وتزيد من سرعتك انت بمعني اصح تأخد من سرعه العدو وتزيدك انت 

مخلب الدماء (بنفسجي اللون) 
عند استخدامها علي احدي الاعداء تأخد من نقاط حياته ويزداد نقاط حياتك لفتره قصيره من الوقت 

مخلب الهجوم (احمر اللون) الافضل للمواجهات واحد لواحد او اكثر تعمل علي اخذ جزئ من ضرر العدو وزياده ضررك الجسدي او السحري 

الثلاث انواع من المخلب عند استخدامها علي عدو تسبب له ضرر حقيقي قد يبدو غير ملحوظ لكن تظهر قوتها عند اخذ السلحفاه او اللورد وهذه هيا الوظيفه الأساسية للمخلب

hero.hero_overview: Dyrroth is a fighter hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang known as the "Prince of the Abyss". He excels at dealing high melee burst damage, especially when his passive rage buff is active, making him effective at chasing and eliminating squishy targets. Dyrroth is a strong lane bully in the EXP lane and can also be played as a jungler, utilizing his mobility and wave clear for split pushing and ambushing enemies.

dominants.dominant_desc:
In teamfights, he should focus on taking out vulnerable backline targets (marksmen, mages). He can strategically use his second skill to reduce enemy defense, then quickly follow up with his first and ultimate skills for burst damage against squishy heroes. Avoiding enemy crowd control as much as possible and knowing when to use his ultimate skill is crucial.

Here's a more detailed breakdown:
1. Targeting Weak Backline Heroes:
His strength lies in his ability to deal high damage to squishy heroes like mages and marksmen, especially when their defense is weakened by his second skill. Look for opportunities to snipe out isolated enemies.
2. Time Your Ultimate:
Derot's ultimate is a powerful burst damage tool that can guarantee a kill or significantly weaken an enemy.
Use his ultimate to surprise the enemy with a kill.
4. Be mindful of enemy crowd control and position yourself flexibly.
5. Coordinate with Your Team:
Communicate with your team in advance to coordinate attacks and maximize damage output. Focus Your Fire: Focus your attacks. Start fights strategically.

emblems.emblem_title: Assassin emblem to get high physical damage

equips.equip_desc: This set of equipment is more powerful and effective for this hero, allowing for better burst damage and sustained engagements. It can deal extremely high physical damage to enemies, and due to its high physical attack, some enemies may be defeated in a single blow. It can also be used to instantly kill squishy enemies.

hero.hero_overview: Dyrroth is a Critical Hit Warrior hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, known for his aggressive and aggression-heavy combat style and his ability to weaken enemy physical defenses. He excels at cutting into enemy lanes and, with his skillful skillset, can easily eliminate squishy enemies. He can be extremely effective in certain team compositions, especially when paired with heroes that also benefit from defense weakening. He's a very powerful hero.
hero.hero_strength:
This hero's strength lies in his extremely high critical damage output, particularly his burst damage. A single skill set can instantly kill squishy heroes, forcing enemy marksmen and mages to retreat.
His potential and ability allow him to quickly deal damage to multiple enemies in a short period of time, sustaining and sustaining damage. He excels at directly splitting lanes, helping to suppress other lanes, and dealing damage to high-priority targets in teamfights.
hero.hero_weakness:
Hero Weaknesses
In Mobile Legends, this hero can be countered by heroes with high mobility, strong burst damage, and/or crowd control abilities, especially in the early game. His sustainability also relies on staying close to minions, making it difficult for him to consistently deal damage against heroes with ranged damage or the ability to escape from the battlefield. Overall, he's a tough opponent against enemy heroes with long-range burst damage, and once controlled, he's difficult to escape.

spell.spell_desc:
Spell Description
In Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, his spell, "Petrify," is a combat spell that allows him to instantly move to an enemy's side after using a movement skill. Using Petrify petrifies nearby enemies.

It lasts for 0.8 seconds, stunning them and reducing their movement speed by 50% for the same duration. It deals 100 points of damage (+15 x hero level).

Petrify deals magic damage to affected enemies. Very useful!

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Franco's Iron Hook (S1) and Bloody Hunt (S3) can isolate a key target (like the enemy EXP laner Dyrroth counters) and lock them in place for Dyrroth's full burst.

Atlas's Ultimate, Fatal Links, groups enemies, allowing Dyrroth to dive into the vulnerable mess, apply the defense shred, and immediately start cleaving. Atlas provides both initiation and protection.
Tigreal's Flicker-Ultimate combo is the perfect setup. The enemy is stunned for over a second, allowing Dyrroth to safely dash in, apply his S2, and execute his target with his Ultimate.

counters.counter_desc: Argus's Ultimate, Eternal Evil, grants him 5 seconds of death immunity, which completely negates the finishing potential of Dyrroth's Abysm Strike (S3).

dominants.dominant_desc: Dyrroth's dominant phase is his early to mid-game, where he functions as a fearsome lane dominator and 1v1 duelist. His power spike is immediate in the laning phase, where his kit allows him to make advantageous trades due to the potent armor reduction from his Spectre Step (S2) and the sustain from his passive. This aggressive early game is crucial for him to snowball by farming aggressively, winning his lane, and starting rotations to gank other lanes. However, his strength begins to diminish as the game drags on, as his enemies complete their defensive items, and in late-game team fights, Dyrroth can be easily shut down by concentrated crowd control (CC) or burst damage before he can execute his full combo.

emblems.emblem_desc: The best emblem set for Dyrroth is the Custom Assassin Emblem, as it perfectly complements his aggressive, high-burst duelist playstyle by maximizing his damage and mobility. You should select Agility for increased movement speed to aid in rotation and closing distances, Master Assassin to boost his physical attack when engaging a single target in his lane, and finally, the key talent Lethal Ignition in the third tier. This primary talent applies a powerful burning effect after his full skill combo, ensuring maximum execution power and capitalizing on the massive armor reduction inflicted by his second skill to secure kills.

equips.equip_desc: Dyrroth's optimal item build balances his core role as a high-burst Physical Damage hero with necessary mid-to-late-game durability, utilizing the maximum benefit from his armor-shredding second skill. The build starts with Tough or Warrior Boots for early defense, followed by Hunter Strike and Blade of the Heptaseas as core damage items; Hunter Strike provides penetration, cooldown reduction, and mobility, while Heptaseas maximizes his initial burst with its passive. For his major power spike, Blade of Despair (BoD) is essential, offering the highest raw physical attack and an execution boost to ensure his Ultimate, Abysm Strike, finishes off low-HP targets. The final two items are typically defensive: Dominance Ice provides anti-heal, defense, and cooldown reduction, and Immortality offers a crucial late-game resurrection for a second chance in team fights.

hero.hero_overview: Dyrroth, the Prince of the Abyss, is a high-burst Fighter who excels in the early to mid-game with his incredible lane presence and armor-shredding capabilities. His passive, Wrath of the Abyss, allows him to deal massive AoE damage and sustain himself through basic attacks, which empower his basic skills for even more burst. He is an aggressive lane bully, particularly effective against tankier heroes due to the Physical Defense reduction on his second skill, Spectre Step.
hero.hero_strength: Dyrroth can easily dominate the Exp Lane from level 1. His passive, combined with Burst Strike (S1), allows for strong poke and wave clear. His Spectre Step (S2)'s second phase, Fatal Strike, reduces the target's Physical Defense by up to 75% for 4 seconds, making him an ideal counter for heroes who rely on armor. The healing from his passive, Circle Strike, allows him to win long trades and recover HP by attacking enemies and minions. His Ultimate, Abysm Strike (S3), is a high-damage, charging attack that deals extra damage based on the enemy's lost HP, making it a perfect execution tool.
hero.hero_weakness: Dyrroth is highly vulnerable to Crowd Control (CC), which easily interrupts his combos and his Ultimate, making him an easy target to burst down. He can also fall off in the late game if he doesn't snowball, as his aggressive, close-range style becomes very risky against grouped enemies. Finally, his Ultimate is predictable, featuring a slow, linear wind-up that agile enemies can easily dodge with a dash or Flicker.

data.recommend: A comprehensive guide for Dyrroth, the Prince of the Abyss! Learn how to dominate the EXP Lane and effectively use his ultimate 75% Physical Defense Reduction to eliminate any enemy tank or fighter.

spell.spell_desc: Provides vital mobility for both offense and defense. Dyrroth lacks a reliable escape when his S2 is on cooldown or used for engagement.  Techniques: Ultimate Extension/Set-up: Charge Abysm Strike (S3), then immediately use Flicker to reposition the skill and catch a retreating or unsuspecting enemy for the kill. This makes his ultimate almost impossible to dodge.

Dyrroth ★★★★★ Huge Advantage Dyrroth has a significant advantage against squishy, immobile heroes, particularly marksmen and mages, due to his high burst damage and gap-closing abilities. Heroes like Layla, Miya, Chang'e, or Eudora struggle to escape Dyrroth's engage and can be quickly eliminated before they can react. His ultimate, Abysm Strike, also reduces physical defense, making him very effective against heroes who rely on physical defense items or those with naturally lower defenses. He can also overwhelm some sustain-focused heroes if he lands his full combo, preventing them from healing effectively.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Laning Phase (Early Game)
 
- Leveling Focus: Prioritize reaching level 4 as quickly as possible. Dyrroth's ultimate ability, Abysm Strike, significantly enhances his damage output and mobility.
- Aggressive Laning: Use Dyrroth's skills to harass the enemy laner and push the lane. His Burst Strike and Spectre Step can be used to poke enemies and clear minion waves efficiently.
- Jungle Awareness: Keep an eye on the enemy jungler's movements. If the enemy jungler is ganking other lanes, use this opportunity to push your lane and take the tower.
- Turtle Control: Help your team secure the first Turtle. Dyrroth's damage can be crucial in taking down the Turtle quickly.

Mid-Game Transition
 
- Objective Focus: Transition from laning to focusing on objectives such as towers and buffs.
Okay, here's the text from the image, continuing the Battle Stage section for Dyrroth:
 
- Ganking Opportunities: Look for opportunities to gank other lanes, especially those where your teammates are struggling. Dyrroth's mobility and burst damage make him an effective ganker.
- Team Fight Positioning: In team fights, focus on eliminating the enemy's damage dealers (marksman or mage). Use Dyrroth's Spectre Step to engage quickly and Burst Strike to deal high damage.
- Tower Pushing: After winning a team fight, push the nearest tower to gain a strategic advantage.
 
Team Fight Strategies
 
- Engage Wisely: Don't engage in team fights without proper vision or backup from your team.
- Burst Damage: Use Dyrroth's ultimate, Abysm Strike, to deal massive damage to key targets.
- Sustain: Utilize Dyrroth's passive, Wrath of the Abyss, to sustain through prolonged fights.

- Peel for Carries: If your team's carries are being targeted, use Dyrroth's skills to protect them and disrupt the enemy's engage.
 
Laning Precautions
 
- Mana Management: Be mindful of Dyrroth's mana consumption, especially in the early game. Avoid spamming skills unnecessarily.
- Gank Awareness: Watch out for ganks from the enemy jungler. Use the mini-map to track their movements and avoid overextending.
- Item Build Adjustments: Adjust your item build based on the enemy team composition. If the enemy team has high physical defense, consider building items like Malefic Roar.

emblems.emblem_desc: The Assassin Emblem is ideal for Dyrroth, enhancing his damage output and mobility. The added Adaptive Attack boosts his physical damage, crucial for bursting down enemies. HP and Mana Regen provides sustain, allowing Dyrroth to stay in the jungle longer without needing to recall frequently. This emblem set ensures Dyrroth has the necessary stats to dominate the early to mid-game and snowball his lead effectively.

equips.equip_desc: This build focuses on maximizing Dyrroth's burst damage potential while providing some survivability. Blade of the Hepta Seas and Blade of Despair significantly increase his physical attack, allowing him to quickly eliminate squishy targets. Endless Battle enhances his sustain and provides true damage, while Queen's Wings and Immortality ensure he can withstand some damage in team fights. Warrior Boots offer mobility and some defense. This build is designed to help Dyrroth dominate the early to mid-game and remain a threat in the late game.

hero.hero_overview: Dyrroth is a Fighter hero specializing in charge and burst damage, making him a formidable force in the early to mid-game. Known for his aggressive playstyle, Dyrroth excels at diving into the backlines, disrupting key targets, and securing quick kills. His abilities allow him to sustain in fights and turn the tide in team battles.
hero.hero_strength: Dyrroth's strengths lie in his early to mid-game dominance. He can quickly clear jungle camps, gank lanes effectively, and snowball his advantage. His enhanced skills provide significant burst damage and control, making him a threat to squishy heroes. Dyrroth also has good sustain due to his passive, which allows him to regenerate HP while dealing damage.
hero.hero_weakness: Dyrroth's effectiveness falls off in the late game when enemies have farmed defensive items. He is also susceptible to crowd control, which can interrupt his combos and limit his mobility. Dyrroth struggles against heroes with strong disengage abilities or those who can kite him effectively.
